meta.disclaimer: Rule-based helper — matches your question against a curated knowledge base. No AI service.

intents.answer: Namaste! I'm the Radar Assistant. Ask me about areas in Kathmandu, Lalitpur or Bhaktapur, typical rents, room types, or how the fair-price score works.

In Kathmandu, RoomRadar lists rooms in Baneshwor, Koteshwor, Kalanki, Chabahil, Baluwatar, Maharajgunj, Kirtipur, Balaju, Gongabu, Samakhusi, Naxal and Thamel. Baneshwor, Naxal and Thamel tend to be pricier; Kirtipur and Kalanki are more affordable.

In Lalitpur (Patan), we cover Kupondole, Jhamsikhel, Pulchowk, Satdobato, Lagankhel, Imadol, Ekantakuna, Sanepa and Bhaisepati. Jhamsikhel and Sanepa are premium; Satdobato and Imadol are gentler on the budget.
In Bhaktapur, RoomRadar lists Suryabinayak, Kamalbinayak, Sallaghari, Dudhpati, Katunje, Sipadol, Thimi and Gatthaghar. Bhaktapur is generally the most affordable of the three cities.
A 1BHK in the Valley typically runs Rs 10,000–20,000 / month, depending on area and amenities. Post it on RoomRadar and our fair-price model tells you if a listing is priced fairly.
2BHK units usually range Rs 16,000–32,000 / month. Central Kathmandu and Jhamsikhel sit at the higher end; Bhaktapur and outer areas are lower.
A single room generally costs Rs 5,000–12,000 / month. Hostels (per seat) are Rs 6,000–13,000. Great for students — try Kirtipur, Kalanki, or Bhaktapur for value.
A full flat typically ranges Rs 22,000–55,000 / month depending on size, floor and location. Sanepa, Jhamsikhel, Baluwatar and Maharajgunj lean expensive.
Hostel seats usually go for Rs 6,000–13,000 / month, meals sometimes included. Popular near Kirtipur (Tribhuvan University) and Pulchowk (engineering campus).
RoomRadar covers five types: Single Room, 1BHK, 2BHK, Flat, and Hostel. (BHK = Bedroom-Hall-Kitchen — so a 1BHK has one bedroom plus a hall and kitchen.)

Every listing is scored by our fair-rent model, which learned from Valley listings. It compares the asking rent to a predicted fair rent and shows a badge: Below market, Fair price, Slightly high, or Above fair — so you instantly know if a room is a good deal.
To post a room: register as an Owner, then go to “Post a room”. As you fill in the details, the model shows a live suggested rent so you can price it fairly. You can add photos too.
Use the search bar or the Explore page. You can filter by city, area, room type, price range and amenities. Results show on a list and an interactive map, each with a fair-price badge.
Listings can include Water Supply, Parking, Attached Bathroom, Wi-Fi Ready, Kitchen and Balcony. You can filter for the ones you need — parking and attached bathroom usually add the most to rent.

For students, Kirtipur (near Tribhuvan University) and Pulchowk (near IOE) are the best-value picks. Hostels run Rs 6,000–13,000 / month; single rooms Rs 5,000–12,000. Filter by “Hostel” or “Single Room” on Explore.
Owners in the Valley typically ask 1–2 months' rent as a refundable deposit, plus the first month upfront. Confirm on the listing page and always get a written receipt.

Utilities (electricity, water, internet) are usually billed separately unless the listing says “inclusive”. Check the amenities list — Wi-Fi Ready means the wiring is there, not always that internet cost is included.
Contact the owner via the listing page to schedule a viewing. It's a good idea to visit in person before paying any deposit — always meet the owner and see the exact room.

Every owner registers before posting, so listings carry a “Verified owner” tag. If something looks off (price way below market, no photos, pressure to pay before viewing), flag it — the admin team reviews reports.
Click “Get started” to register as a Seeker (to browse) or an Owner (to post rooms). It's free.
Right now RoomRadar focuses on the Kathmandu Valley — Kathmandu, Lalitpur and Bhaktapur. More cities are on our future roadmap.
For support, use the Contact link in the footer. For listing questions, the fair-price badge and this assistant cover most of what you'll need.
RoomRadar is a final-year B.Sc. CSIT project by Bishow Lamichhane and Anjan Sharma — a fair-rent finder for the Kathmandu Valley powered by a regression model trained on real Valley listings.
Happy to help! Ask me anything else about rooms, areas or pricing.

fallback: I'm not sure about that one — I can help with areas (Kathmandu, Lalitpur, Bhaktapur), typical rents, room types, amenities, how to post or search, and how the fair-price score works. Try one of the suggestions below.
